item.title: Kastom Giraun celebrates the importance of customary land
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Kastom Giraun is the title of a new book and website launched by ACT NOW! to help teach young people on the value and importance of customary land.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: Special Economic Zones move from concept to reality
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The creation of Special Economic Zones is one of the PNG Government’s key strategies for encouraging economic growth. We talk to the acting head of PNG’s Special Economic Zones Authority to understand the opportunity SEZs represent to investors. The post Special Economic Zones move from concept to r

item.title: Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform
item.description: [Education News PNG] APIA, SAMOA – 16 February 2026 – The Pacific Academy of Sciences (PAS) today announced two historic milestones for Pacific research and innovation at its The post Pacific Academy Unveils AI-Powered Research Platform first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Inflation Ticks Up as Australians Wait for May Budget
item.description: [Inside PNG] Rising inflation in January has put the Australian Federal Budget and interest rates in the spotlight as housing and food costs drive underlying prices to 3.4%. The post Inflation Ticks Up as Australians Wait for May Budget first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Inflation Ticks Up as Australians Wa

item.title: PNG celebrates Alex Johnston’s NRL record as PM Marape praises historic milestone
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has joined rugby league fans around the world in celebrating the historic moment when Alex Johnston became the highest try-scorer in National Rugby League history. The achievement came during the South Sydney Rabbitohs’ match against the Sydney Roosters at Allianz Stadium in Sydney,

item.title: Alex Johnston on the Brink of Rugby League History
item.description: [Pawa TV] Alex Johnston of the South Sydney Rabbitohs is poised to break Ken Irvine's long-standing rugby league try-scoring record, representing a significant achievement for Papua New Guinea and the sport's history. The post Alex Johnston on the Brink of Rugby League History first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG50 Gospel Music Festival set for August
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Jubilee Reset Festival: A Nation Gathers to Worship, Pray, and Reset for the Future As Papua New Guinea approaches its 50th year of Independence, a powerful wave of faith and unity is building across the nation. At the heart of this movement is the Jubilee Reset Gospel Music Festival, taking place o

item.title: PNG PM Marape congratulates Alex Johnston on historic NRL try-scoring record
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has congratulated National Rugby League star Alex Johnston after the winger created history by becoming the highest try-scorer in NRL history. Johnston reached the milestone of 213 career tries during South Sydney Rabbitohs’ clash with the Sydney Roosters

item.title: Madang small craft board urges owners to register
item.description: [Post Courier] Boat owners in Madang Province are being urged to register their vessels as the Madang Provincial Small Craft Board continues its registration exercise for small boats. The Madang Small Craft Board and Registry Office was officially launched on December 5, 2025, and the registration process began on

item.title: Prime Minister Marape Congratulates Alex Johnston on Historic NRL Try Scoring Record
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea: Prime Minister Hon. James Marape has congratulated National Rugby League (NRL) star Alex Johnston following his historic achievement of becoming the highest Try-Scorer in NRL history, reaching 213 Career Tries and surpassing the long-standing record previously held by

item.title: PNG signs K1 billion deal for major Cement Project
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has welcomed the signing of the Project Development Agreement (PDA) for the Central Lime and Cement Project in Central Province. He described it as a major step towards strengthening the country’s manufacturing base, reducing import dependence, and lowering construction

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: Kastom Giraun: Celebrating the importance of customary land
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Kastom Giraun: New book and website celebrate the importance of customary land

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: Papua New Guinea’s orchids on show in new garden
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Mary Tao looks at the new exhibit at the Port Moresby Nature Park featuring a collection of Papua New Guinea's stunning local orchids. The post Papua New Guinea’s orchids on show in new garden appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.description: [Education News PNG] From the village of Nanuku on Malo Island, Vanuatu, to the global arena of diplomacy and international affairs, Lorenzo Moli’s academic and professional journey is The post Lorenzo Moli’s Path to Global Affairs first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Cricket PNG Sets Ambitious Goal to Reach 150,000 Youth in 2026
item.description: [Inside PNG] Cricket PNG sets a bold 2026 goal to reach 150,000 youth, aligning regional managers and high-performance plans at the Port Moresby conference. The post Cricket PNG Sets Ambitious Goal to Reach 150,000 Youth in 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Cricket PNG Sets Ambitious Goal to Reach 150

item.title: PNG Rugby League Community Hails Johnston’s Historic 213-Try Milestone
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea rugby league fans were among thousands celebrating worldwide after South Sydney winger Alex Johnston set a new NRL record with his 213rd career try on Friday night. The landmark moment occurred during the Rabbitohs’ match at Allianz Stadium when Johnston crossed in the 41st minute t

item.title: The Kalam Headdress
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's cultural diversity is epitomised by traditional headdresses, particularly the Kalam mimor beetle headdress, which signifies identity, prestige, and cultural continuity amidst modern challenges threatening its preservation. The post The Kalam Headdress first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG NRL Competition finalise Club Line-Up for 2025 Season
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The PNG NRL Competition has announced that the 12 existing teams will compete for the top spot in the 2025 Digicel ExxonMobil Cup, starting in April of 2025. After thoroughly reviewing the 2024 season and assessing the potential for expansion, it was concluded that the competition in 2025 will featu

item.title: PNG City Watch Officers Attacked in Broad Daylight Near Vision City, Four Suspects Detained
item.description: [PNG Facts] Four male suspects are being held at the Six Mile Police Station following a violent attack on Port Moresby City Watch officers near the overhead bridge linking Vision City and Range View shopping centres in Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea, on Thursday. PNG City Watch Officers Attacked in Broad Dayli

item.title: Upper Buang to be powered soon
item.description: [Post Courier] Over 20 hamlets spread across six ward areas in the upper Buang area will soon have electricity supply restored fully. This electricity supply restoration exercise was undertaken by the Bulolo Development Authority through it’s Bulolo District Rural Electrification Project in 2024 and is finally nea

item.title: Oil Palm chairmen back National Oil Palm Bill
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Chairmen of Oil Palm Growers Associations from five declared projects under the Oil Palm Industry Corporation (OPIC) have united in Port Moresby to reaffirm their strong support for the two National Oil Palm Bills and reject claims made by five individual oil palm growers from the Hoskins Project in

item.title: PM Marape backs Bipartisan Land Reform Report, recommendations
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has welcomed and strongly supported the Parliamentary Committee Report on Land, presented in Parliament today by the Chairman of the Customary Land and Land Reforms Committee, Keith Iduhu, Member for Hiri-Koiari. PM Marape described it as a timely and critical step towar

item.title: PNGFA’s weak response to log export transparency call
item.description: [Act Now PNG] PNGFA has responded weakly to ACT NOW's call for transparency on log export data

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: Port Moresby hotels spend big ahead of anticipated visitor influx
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Arrivals into Port Moresby are expected to grow substantially in the coming years. Business Advantage PNG provides a roundup of the big investments being made by PNG’s hotel operators in anticipation of this growth. item.title: Renovation Work Commences at Insect Collection Centre
item.description: [Inside PNG] Renovation begins at the NAIC in Port Moresby. The K210,000 project upgrades storage for 96,000+ insect specimens to boost agricultural research. The post Renovation Work Commences at Insect Collection Centre first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Renovation Work Commences at Insect Collection Cent

item.title: PNG Governor Parkop Questions MPs’ Power to Elect Prime Minister
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop has raised concerns in Parliament about whether members of Parliament should continue holding the authority to elect the Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea. During a debate on constitutional reform yesterday, the governor said the country must examine

item.title: How Governments Are Formed After Elections
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea is a parliamentary democracy where the National Parliament comprises 111 elected members. Election processes lead to coalition negotiations, followed by the election of a Prime Minister and Cabinet formation. The post How Governments Are Formed After Elections first appeared on PAWA

item.title: Operation Render Safe set to begin in East New Britain
item.description: [PNG Buzz] This week, Governor Michael Marum and Provincial Administrator Levi Mano welcomed Australian High Commissioner John Feakes, Defence Adviser Colonel Travis Gordon, and a team from the Australian Defence Force (ADF) in East New Britain. This visit marks the beginning of a series to the province, as th

item.title: Rugby League World Applauds PNG's Johnston After Record-Breaking 213th Try
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea rugby league fans joined supporters across the sport in celebrating a historic moment after South Sydney winger Alex Johnston became the NRL’s all-time leading tryscorer with his 213th career try on Friday night. The milestone came during the Rabbitohs’ clash at Allianz Stadium when

item.title: Police reminded to maintain one legal spouse
item.description: [Post Courier] Members of the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary have been reminded to comply with the organisation’s standing orders requiring officers to maintain only one legally recognised spouse. The reminder was issued by Director of the Human Resource Directorate, Superintendent Paul Unupite, during the cl

item.title: Wabo Set for 50th game milestone on Sunday
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO SP PNG Hunters dynamic fullback Sanny Wabo will celebrate his 50th appearance in the QRL Hostplus Cup when the Hunters take on the Wynnum Manly Seagulls in round two at the Santos National Football Stadium in Port Moresby this Sunday. Wabo, a fan favourite known for his skill, spee

item.title: Nomane-LingStuckey showdown over grey listing and Kina drop
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Opposition Leader and Member for Chuave Open, James Nomane, has taken the Government and Treasurer Ian Ling-Stuckey over the country’s economic management with regards to greylisting and declining Kina. He accused the Treasurer of failing to act on warnings that led to the country being placed on th

item.title: PMGH laik ol femili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: Call on PNG Forest Authority to publish log export data
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calling on the PNG Forest Authority to publish the complete log export data for 2024 and 2025.

item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

PLC Managing Director Paul Mulder tells Business Advantage PNG why these agreements are so important to the success of its Central Cement and Lime Project. The post Key milestones

item.description: [Education News PNG] A PhD scholar at The University of the South Pacific (USP) has made significant contributions to Pacific freshwater ecology, co-authoring the discovery and documentation of The post USP scholar advances contributions to Pacific Freshwater Ecology first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Huon-Gulf Communities Receive Water Tanks
item.description: [Inside PNG] Huon-Gulf rollout: Local MP and Minister Jason Peter delivers 1,000L water tanks to villages, turning 2024 roofing projects into vital rainwater systems. item.title: PNG Bilums Return Home After Strong Start in Oceania Qualifiers
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The Papua New Guinea (PNG) Women’s National Football Team, known as the Bilums, returned to Port Moresby today after a successful campaign at the FIFA Women’s World Cup Oceania Qualifiers held in Fiji. PNG impressed throughout the group stage, securing three consecutive victories to finish on top of

item.title: Snakebite Antivenom in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant challenges in accessing life-saving antivenom for snakebite treatment, with rural facilities often lacking supplies despite improvements in distribution and partnerships. Access disparities increase health risks. The post Snakebite Antivenom in PNG first appeared o

item.title: PNG Delegation learns from Madagascar’s Vanilla Industry
item.description: [PNG Buzz] A delegation from Papua New Guinea, including senior officers from central government agencies such as the Department of National Planning and Monitoring (DNPM), the Spice Industry Board (SIB) under the Department of Agriculture and Livestock (DAL), as well as Provincial Government and Administratio

item.title: PNG Police Chief Warns Public Against Prank Calls to National Call Centre
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Police Commissioner David Manning has warned citizens not to make prank calls to the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary’s National Call Centre, saying such behaviour could delay responses to real emergencies. Speaking during the launch of the new call centre in Port Moresby, Mr Man

item.title: Technology upgrade drives new Police Emergency Coordination Centre
item.description: [Post Courier] The launch of the new national police call centre marks a major step in the digital transformation of the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary. Police Commissioner David Manning said the facility is part of the force’s wider digitisation program designed to strengthen coordination and improve respons

item.title: Hunters coach highlights challenges in player development
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO SP PNG Hunters head coach Paul Aiton says his coaching staff is facing challenges in grooming new players, particularly in teaching basic skills that should already have been mastered at the Digicel ExxonMobil Cup franchise level. Following the Hunters’ 16–24 loss to the Rockhampto

item.title: Commissioner Manning applauds police crackdown on crime in Enga
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Commissioner of Police, David Manning, has commended the Provincial Police Command in Enga for its ongoing heightened arrest rate that has seen 79 arrests in the past two days, with many more expected. Commissioner Manning in a statement said despite being given the opportunity to give up their weap

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Call on PNG Forest Authority to Publish Latest Log Export Data
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calling on the PNG Forest Authority to publish the complete log export data for 2024 and 2025.

item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.title: Bautama Dream Takes Root as Rosso Pushes for K100,000 Homes
item.description: [Inside PNG] Acting PM John Rosso launches Bautama projects, reviving Robert Agarobe’s vision for a Central City with affordable K100,000 housing for workers. The post Bautama Dream Takes Root as Rosso Pushes for K100,000 Homes first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bautama Dream Takes Root as Rosso Pushes for

item.title: PNG PM Marape Launches K382.5 Million Passam–Angoram Road Project
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape has launched the K382.5 million design and construction project for the 87-kilometre road linking Passam Junction to Angoram Station in East Sepik Province. The announcement was made during a project launch at the Angoram waterfront on Friday, March 6, ma

item.title: Leatherback Turtles in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea hosts six of the world's seven sea turtle species, including the declining Leatherback turtles. Community conservation efforts and international partnerships aim to protect these endangered species amid ongoing threats. The post Leatherback Turtles in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Bougainville chocolate festival 2023 underway
item.description: [PNG Buzz] The 2023 annual Bougainville Chocolate Festival is now underway, held over three days from the 26 to 28 September at the Hutjena Oval in Buka. It will be the largest event since the inaugural festival in 2016. The theme of this year’s festival is “Innovation and Information Sharing to Empower Farmer

item.title: 274 Students Graduate from PNG's Divine Word University Port Moresby Campus
item.description: [PNG Facts] A total of 274 students graduated from the Port Moresby Campus of Divine Word University during the institution’s 44th graduation ceremony, marking another milestone for higher education in Papua New Guinea. The ceremony in the nation’s capital brought together graduates, families, lecturers, alumni

item.title: Turi orders Zero Tolerance on offensive weapons
item.description: [Post Courier] Police in the nation’s capital have been directed to step up enforcement operations with immediate effect, focusing on removing offensive weapons from public transport operators, street vendors and individuals in public spaces. Acting Assistant Commissioner of Police (NCD/Central) Ben Turi has instr

item.title: Benji Kot returns from Injury to boost Hunters in 2026 Season
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO SP PNG Hunters centre and second-rower Benji Kot has made his return to the field for the 2026 Queensland Rugby League (QRL) Hostplus Cup season, after recovering from an anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injury sustained last year. Kot, known as one of the Hunters’ hard-running sec

item.title: PM Marape assures completion of Madang Airport Terminal
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape this morning assured the people of Madang Province that the National Government will intervene if necessary to ensure the long-delayed Madang Airport terminal is completed, following the abandonment of the project by the contractor responsible for its construction. PM Ma

item.title: K30.9 bilien baset
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavman i tokaut long K30.9 bilien baset bilong kantri long 2026 tasol em bai no inap yet long bungim ol nid na laik bilong ol manmeri

item.title: What's in a bilum?: PNG's iconic string bag
item.description: [Act Now PNG] In Papua New Guinea, the bilum is among the most familiar objects of daily life.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: On the grey list, forex supply and the impact of war
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Papua New Guinea's greylisting by the Financial Action Task Force, the potential re-emergence of forex supply issues and the trickle-down effects of war in the Middle East are all reminders that PNG is increasingly connected to the global economy – for better or worse. The post On the grey list, for

item.title: “MY BABY MATTERED”: Mother Speaks Out Over Newborn’s Death at Angau Hospital
item.description: [Inside PNG] Elizabeth Narun alleges systemic failure at Angau Hospital after the tragic death of her baby. A mother’s plea for healthcare reform in PNG. The post “MY BABY MATTERED”: Mother Speaks Out Over Newborn’s Death at Angau Hospital first appeared on Inside PNG . The post “MY BABY MATTERED”: Mother Speaks

item.title: PNG Hekari FC Claim Historic First Win in OFC Pro League
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea champions PNG Hekari FC have etched their name into the record books after securing their first-ever win in the OFC Pro League with a hard-fought 3-1 triumph over Vanuatu United. The result marks a major milestone for the PNG club, which had been searching for a breakthrough victory

item.title: The Origins of Buai
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Betel Nut, or buai, is a culturally significant psychoactive substance in PNG, used in rituals and social events. However, its consumption poses serious health risks and environmental issues, prompting calls for balance. The post The Origins of Buai first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Agripreneurs empowered, MSMEs boosted in Sepik Region
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Under the EU-STREIT PNG programme, FAO equips local entrepreneurs with knowledge, skills and practical tools needed to drive inclusive sustainable development for MSMEs in the Sepik region. Wewak, Papua New Guinea – The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), as part of the EU

item.title: PNG Prime Minister James Marape Pushes Reform to End Polygamy
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Prime Minister James Marape says his government is considering a new law that would gradually bring an end to polygamy in Papua New Guinea as part of efforts to promote gender equality and protect women. During parliamentary discussions focused on women’s issues and family welfare,

item.title: RPNGC launches national 112 call centre for faster emergency response
item.description: [Post Courier] The Commissioner of Police, David Manning, launched the new RPNGC National Call Centre, providing a streamlined national call centre for 112 calls to police. Staffed by a specialist dispatch team and police personnel, the National Call Centre enhances coordination of emergency response, providing a

item.title: MP Calls for National Trauma Healing Program
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] The Regional Member for Autonomous Region of Bougainville, Peter Tsiamalili Jr., has called on the National Government to establish a national trauma healing program to address the lasting emotional and psychological scars of the Bougainville Crisis. Speaking in Parliament this week, Mr Tsiamalili s

item.title: PM Marape outlines National Airport upgrades, pays tribute to Somare Government for modern Airports
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has outlined the Government’s ongoing nationwide airport infrastructure upgrades while paying tribute to the government of the late Grand Chief Sir Michael Somare and former Works, Transport and Civil Aviation Minister Don Polye for initiating the programme that moderniz

item.title: Bird sanap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Gavana bilong Is Sepik provins i sanap makim oposisen long traim na winim James Marape long kamap Praim Minista insait long 'Vot i no gat bilip'.

item.title: UN questions PNG Government on SABL leases and illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] UN is calling for answers from the PNG government over its controversial SABL leases and the use of FCAs as cover for illegal logging operations.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Future-proofing Papua New Guinea’s ports
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] With a major program of port upgrades underway, and a new focus on investing in facilities that support business growth, PNG’s key ports look set to become drivers of greater prosperity. Neil Papenfus, CEO of PNG Ports Corporation, shares the plan with Business Advantage PNG. The post Future-proofin

item.description: [Education News PNG] In an era defined by technological advancement and climate realities, young Pacific engineers are stepping forward as drivers of transformative change. Among them is Mrunmayi The post Mrunmayi’s Final-Year Engineering Project Delivers Smart Hydration System first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Angau Hospital Hit by Critical Medicine Shortage and Overcrowding
item.description: [Inside PNG] Lae resident Joshua Kepas reports critical medicine shortages and dire overcrowding at Angau Hospital, forcing families to buy their own supplies. The post Angau Hospital Hit by Critical Medicine Shortage and Overcrowding first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Angau Hospital Hit by Critical Medicin

item.title: PNG Trade Landscape May See Gradual Rise in Chinese Yuan Use
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea’s trade environment could experience a gradual increase in Chinese Yuan transactions, but the United States dollar will remain a crucial settlement currency, Bank of Papua New Guinea Governor Elizabeth Genia says. PNG Trade Landscape May See Gradual Rise in Chinese Yuan Use She stat

item.title: Kuk Agriculture Site
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Kuk Agricultural Site in Papua New Guinea, dating back over 10,000 years, highlights early farming practices and stands as a significant example of agricultural development in the Pacific region. The post Kuk Agriculture Site first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Showcasing PNG to the world: 2023 Goroka Show a Success
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Papua New Guinea’s rich cultural heritage came alive once again as the 67th Goroka Show took centre stage at the National Sports Institute (NSI) in Goroka, Eastern Highlands Province. Held over the long weekend commemorating PNG’s 48th Independence, this year’s event, sponsored by the National Gamin

item.title: PNG Chiefs official sparks talk after Reece Walsh mention in podcast
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea’s planned NRL franchise, the PNG Chiefs, has drawn attention after a senior club official unexpectedly mentioned Reece Walsh while discussing the team’s future preparations for its entry into the National Rugby League in 2028. PNG Chiefs Football Manager Michael Chammas made the rem

item.title: Seagulls stage stunning comeback to Topple Hunters
item.description: [Post Courier] In a dramatic turnaround at home, the SP PNG Hunters' early dominance evaporated in the second half as the Wynnum Manly Seagulls surged back to secure a 28-20 victory. The post Seagulls stage stunning comeback to Topple Hunters appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: PNG Greens Party condemns ‘human rights ambush’ in West Papua
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] The PNG Greens Party has strongly condemned the continuing and long-standing human rights violations against the indigenous Melanesian people of West Papua. The party believes the current situation in West Papua is nothing short of a “human rights ambush” where innocent indigenous communities are tr

item.title: Ten Officers secure permanent roles in DPLGA Public Service
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Provincial and Local Level Government Affairs (DPLGA) last Thursday officially welcomed ten of its officers into the Papua New Guinea Public Service, presenting them with letters of permanency. The letters were issued by the Human Resource Management (HRM) Division. Acting HRM Dire

item.title: Urbanization in Melanesia
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Urbanisation in Melanesia is a rapidly growing trend and the changes come with their own challenges.

item.title: UN Committee questions government on SABL leases and illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] UNCERD is calling for answers from the government over its cancellation of SABL leases and the illegal use of Forest Clearing Authorities

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: Full steam ahead: how the new Papua New Guinea rugby league team is shaping up
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Claire Nathan reports on the countdown to the most significant moment in PNG’s sporting history, the premiere of the PNG Chiefs. The post Full steam ahead: how the new Papua New Guinea rugby league team is shaping up appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Committee to Investigate UOG Incident
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Minister for Higher Education, Research, Science and Technology (HERST) and Sports, Kinoka Feo, said an Independent Review Committee (IRC) has been established to investigate The post Committee to Investigate UOG Incident first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Marape Champions PNG as Prime Investment Frontier at Davos
item.description: [Inside PNG] PM James Marape promotes PNG as a top emerging market, citing 4% growth, low inflation, and a "Friends to all" policy for investors. The post Marape Champions PNG as Prime Investment Frontier at Davos first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Marape Champions PNG as Prime Investment Frontier at Davos

item.title: PNG Tourism Policy Reform Targets Sustainable Growth
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea is reforming its tourism industry to position it as an investment-ready sector capable of driving sustainable economic growth. At a legislative review workshop in Port Moresby, PNG Tourism Promotion Authority CEO Eric Mossman Uvovo said the reforms were crucial to unlocking the sect

item.title: Aid Funding to Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Foreign assistance is crucial for Papua New Guinea's development, supporting infrastructure and governance. However, challenges such as fund mismanagement and aid dependency threaten sustainability and effectiveness. The post Aid Funding to Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Hambini Village’s journey to transformation
item.description: [PNG Buzz] Thanks to community unity, international support by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) and visionary leadership, 9,000+ people of Hambini Village in East Sepik Province not only took a path to improve their economic prospects using cocoa but also paved the way for a br

item.title: PNG Prepares for NRL Era with K200 Million Chiefs Players Village
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea’s drive to establish its presence in the National Rugby League continued with the start of construction on a K200 million residential complex for the PNG Chiefs. The apartment village at Airways will serve as the permanent accommodation base for players, coaching staff and their fam

item.title: Four arrested after armed attack on Port Moresby city watch officers
item.description: [Post Courier] Four suspects have been arrested following a violent armed attack on members of the POM City Watch Response Unit along Range View on Waigani Drive on Saturday morning. The incident occurred at around 11:00am on March 14 when a group of five men arrived in a grey Toyota Vista vehicle and confronted o

item.title: Iduhu Presents Major Customary Land Reform Report to Parliament
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By JOEL K ANDREW CHAIRMAN of the Special Parliamentary Committee on Customary Land and Land Reforms, Keith Iduhu, today presented a comprehensive report to Parliament outlining proposals aimed at strengthening the protection of customary land while allowing fair and sustainable development for Papua

item.title: Give power to elect PM back to citizens, PM should serve full 5-year tenure in office: Governor Parkop
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop has called for major reforms to Papua New Guinea’s political system, questioning whether members of Parliament should continue to hold the power to elect the Prime Minister. Speaking during a parliamentary debate on constitutional reform whether or not

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Still no decisive ban on round log export
item.description: [Act Now PNG] We are enlightened by the forest canopy, and so we take the announcement by the Papua New Guinea Forest Authority to ban round log exports this year with great unease.

item.title: Independence 1 September 2027 :Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting
item.description: [Bougainville News] Opening Statement by ABG President Hon. Ishmael Toroama, MHR at the Joint Consultation Moderator Meeting Sir Jerry Mateparae, Distinguished Moderator, the Honourable James Marape, Prime Minister of Papua New Guinea, Foreign Missions, distinguished dignitaries, ladies and gentlemen. First I would … C

item.title: PNGX Group’s Chairman on acquisition plans, capital market reforms and regional partnerships
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] David Lawrence is chairman of PNGX Group, the owner and operator of Papua New Guinea’s national stock exchange, PNGX. In this interview with Business Advantage PNG, he expands on the proposed acquisition of PNG Registries, potential government reforms, and opportunities to collaborate with Fiji’s SP

item.title: USP Professors Elected as new Fellows of the Pacific Academy of Sciences
item.description: [Education News PNG] Professors Bibhya Sharma and Sushil Kumar, distinguished academics from The University of the South Pacific (USP), are among thirteen leading Pacific researchers and innovators elected The post USP Professors Elected as new Fellows of the Pacific Academy of Sciences first appeared on Education News

item.title: Kai Tane Appointed as New CEO of National St John Ambulance
item.description: [Inside PNG] National St John Ambulance Council appoints Kai Tane as Chief Executive. A veteran paramedic and leader, Tane aims to drive service excellence. The post Kai Tane Appointed as New CEO of National St John Ambulance first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kai Tane Appointed as New CEO of National St Jo

item.title: PNG Government Buys Back M16s and AK47 in Enga Gun Amnesty
item.description: [PNG Facts] The Papua New Guinea Government has begun buying back illegal firearms in Enga Province, with 24 weapons including M16 rifles and an AK47 surrendered to police during a gun amnesty programme in Wabag on Friday. The weapons were handed over by members of several tribal groups as part of a government

item.title: Six city watch officers injured while maintaining order in Waigani
item.description: [Post Courier] Six POM City Watch officers were injured during a violent confrontation with a group of armed suspects while carrying out routine operations along Waigani Drive on Saturday morning. The officers were stationed along Range View when five men arrived in a vehicle and began attacking them using bush kn

item.title: Women Leaders Drive Stronger Justice Institutions in PNG
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Papua New Guinea–Australia partnership continues to support initiatives aimed at strengthening women’s leadership, promoting inclusive institutions, and addressing gender-based barriers across the law and justice sector. These ongoing efforts were highlighted during the Leadership Learning and D

item.title: Minister clarifies DDA role when Parliamentary Seat becomes vacant
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Minister for Provincial and Local-level Government Affairs, Soroi Marepo Eoe has clarified the legal basis and purpose of recent administrative measures taken under the District Development Authority (DDA) Act 2014 when a parliamentary seat becomes vacant. Minister Eoe said the DDA Act supports shor

item.title: NGOs submit Equator Principles complaint against financiers of the Papua LNG project
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Six organisations have submitted the first formal complaint to the Equator Principles detailing urgent concerns about the potential climate, biodiversity and human rights impacts of the proposed Papua LNG project

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Speaker of the House of Representatives Simon Pentanu, has announced the official dates of the 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Speaker of the House of Representatives Simon Pentanu, has announced the official dates of the 2025 ABG General Election Mr Pentanu said that he would be issuing the writs for the 2025 ABG General Election of the Bougainville House of … Continue reading →

item.title: People Moves: Steamships Logistics, Air Niugini, TISA Bank, NiuPay, ANZ PNG, Joint Venture Port Services, PNG Air
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Restructure at Steamships Logistics, new CEO at Air Niugini, new acting CEO at TISA Bank, new COO at NiuPay, new Executive Director Institutional at ANZ PNG, new National Operations Manager at Joint Venture Port Services and more. The post People Moves: Steamships Logistics, Air Niugini, TISA Bank,

item.title: University of Goroka Suspended Indefinitely After Student–Local Violence
item.description: [Education News PNG] GOROKA, Eastern Highlands — The University of Goroka (UOG) has been suspended indefinitely after violent clashes erupted between students and local residents over the weekend, The post University of Goroka Suspended Indefinitely After Student–Local Violence first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ok Tedi Reports K9.3 Billion Revenue Amid Infrastructure Hurdles
item.description: [Inside PNG] Ok Tedi Mining has reported K9.3 billion in 2025 revenue, delivering a K1 billion dividend to PNG while exceeding copper and gold targets. The post Ok Tedi Reports K9.3 Billion Revenue Amid Infrastructure Hurdles first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Ok Tedi Reports K9.3 Billion Revenue Amid Infra

item.title: PNG Former Defence Chiefs Call for Neutrality Statement on Global Conflict
item.description: [PNG Facts] A clear public declaration from the Government of Papua New Guinea on the country’s neutrality has been requested by former commanders of the Papua New Guinea Defence Force following rising tensions between the United States, Israel and Iran. Retired Major General Jerry Singirok and retired Commodor

item.title: Parkop condemns attack on city watch officers
item.description: [Post Courier] National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop has strongly condemned a violent armed attack on members of the POM City Watch (PCW) Response Unit, praising the officers for their professionalism and restraint under fire. "Our City Watch officers put themselves at risk every day to protect the publi

item.title: MITI requests DoWH to establish a SEZ Building Board
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Ministry of International Trade and Investment has requested the Department of Works and Highways (DoWH) to set up a Special Economic Zones (SEZs) Building Board for the country. Minister, Richard Maru, said all SEZs around the world had their own Building Boards to fast-track building permit ap

item.title: Panguna delegation visits Ok Tedi for Mining insights
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A group of Panguna Mine Landowners has visited the Ok Tedi Mining Limited (OTML) to learn insights from the company’s successes. OTML in a statement said as Papua New Guinea’s leading national mining company, they are committed to strengthening communities through open knowledge sharing, practical l

item.title: Video explainer: The FCA Logging Scandal
item.description: [Act Now PNG] New video explains how FCA's are being abused

item.title: PNG Government Boosts Divine Word University with K15m Funding
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has provided a K15 million funding boost to Divine Word University, aimed at improving campus infrastructure and supporting future enrolment growth. PNG Government Boosts Divine Word University with K15m Funding The funding was presented by Higher Education Minister Kinoka Hotune Fe

item.title: Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation

item.title: PNG Bilums Edge Fiji to Top Group B in Women’s World Cup Qualifiers
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea secured top spot in Group B after edging Fiji 1-0 in their final Round 2 match of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 Oceania Qualifiers at Govind Park. PNG Bilums Edge Fiji to Top Group B in Women’s World Cup Qualifiers / Photo credit: OFC Media via Phototek The narrow victory means PN
